segments.text: Some time later God tested Abraham and said to him,
Abraham!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Here I am,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: he answered.
Take your son,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: God said,
your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains, which I will show you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So Abraham got up early the next morning, saddled his donkey, and took along two of his servants and his son Isaac. He split the wood for a burnt offering and set out for the place God had designated.
On the third day Abraham looked up and saw the place in the distance.
Stay here with the donkey,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Abraham told his servants.
The boy and I will go over there to worship, and then we will return to you.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and placed it on his son Isaac. He himself carried the fire and the sacrificial knife, and the two of them walked on together.
Then Isaac said to his father Abraham,
My father!
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Here I am, my son,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: he replied.
The fire and the wood are here,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: said Isaac,
but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Abraham answered,
God Himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering, my son.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And the two walked on together.
When they arrived at the place God had designated, Abraham built the altar there and arranged the wood. He bound his son Isaac and placed him on the altar, atop the wood.
Then Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.
Just then the angel[^99] of the LORD called out to him from heaven,
Abraham, Abraham!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Here I am,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: he replied.
Do not lay a hand on the boy or do anything to him,
segments.speaker: the Angel

segments.text: said the angel,
for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your only son from me.[^100]
segments.speaker: the Angel

segments.text: Then Abraham looked up and saw behind him a ram[^101] in a thicket, caught by its horns. So he went and took the ram and offered it as a burnt offering in place of his son.
And Abraham called that place The LORD Will Provide.[^102] So to this day it is said,
On the mountain of the LORD it will be provided.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And the angel of the LORD called to Abraham from heaven a second time,
saying,
By Myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, that because you have done this and have not withheld your only son,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will surely bless you, and I will multiply your descendants[^103] like the stars in the sky and the sand on the seashore. Your descendants will possess the gates of their enemies.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And through your offspring all nations of the earth will be blessed,[^104] because you have obeyed My voice.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Abraham went back to his servants, and they got up and set out together for Beersheba. And Abraham settled in Beersheba.
Some time later, Abraham was told,
Milcah has also borne sons to your brother Nahor:
segments.speaker: Some time later, Abraham was

segments.text: Uz the firstborn, his brother Buz, Kemuel (the father of Aram),
segments.speaker: Some time later, Abraham was

segments.text: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
segments.speaker: Some time later, Abraham was

segments.text: And Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. Milcah bore these eight sons to Abraham’s brother Nahor.
Moreover, Nahor’s concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
